text.title: TypeScript Strict + isolatedDeclarations + Project References Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ts-strict-isolated methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every tsconfig.json MUST set `"strict": true`. Disabling strict at the project root is forbidden; per-file `// @ts-nocheck` is also forbidden.
rule.rationale: Strict is the floor for AI-generated TS quality; below it, the compiler stops being a safety net.

rule.statement: Every tsconfig.json MUST set `"noUncheckedIndexedAccess": true`. Without it, `arr[i]` is typed `T` instead of `T | undefined` and runtime crashes hide from the compiler.
rule.rationale: AI-generated code routinely indexes arrays without bounds checks; the flag turns that into a compile error.

rule.statement: Every tsconfig.json MUST set `"exactOptionalPropertyTypes": true` so `{ x?: T }` cannot be satisfied by `{ x: undefined }`.
rule.rationale: Without it, `undefined` vs missing-key bugs sneak through; AI-generated payloads often confuse the two.

rule.statement: Libraries / shared packages MUST set `"isolatedDeclarations": true`. Apps may opt out only if the team uses tsc as the sole emitter.
rule.rationale: Without it, fast emitters (Biome/oxc/swc) cannot produce `.d.ts` and the build falls back to slow tsc inference.

rule.statement: Monorepo packages MUST set `"composite": true` and declare `references` to dependency packages. Hand-rolled path mappings without references are forbidden.
rule.rationale: References make build order explicit; path-mapping-only setups silently break on changing dependency graphs.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
